action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 11 fine-tuned the full pretrained MetricGAN+ for 20 epochs with MR-STFT+L1 loss. Validation PESQ degraded monotonically from ~2.85 (epoch 1) down to ~2.33 (epoch 20) — catastrophic forgetting destroyed the PESQ-optimal pretrained weights. The pretrained baseline still holds the best test score of 3.134. Analysis: The pretrained model is already state-of-the-art on VoiceBank+DEMAND (paper ~3.15 PESQ). Generic perceptual losses do not align with the adversarial/PESQ-aware MetricGAN+ objective, so updating all parameters is harmful. A much safer approach is to freeze the pretrained enhancer and train a tiny residual waveform adapter (~36K params) that learns to correct systematic errors without risking catastrophic forgetting. The adapter is initialized to identity (last layer zeros), so it starts exactly at the pretrained performance level and can only improve or stay the same. Decision: 1) Write model.py with a small dilated-conv ResidualAdapter. 2) Rewrite train.py to freeze the pretrained enhancer and train only the adapter with L1 loss. 3) Update predict.py to optionally compose the adapter on top of the pretrained model. 4) Run a 2-epoch smoke test to verify the pipeline starts at the pretrained PESQ baseline and that training works.
